raw_consumer_statement:
Starting XX/XX/XXXX, the following charges began appearing on my card. I use autopay, and only noticed the charges this month. 

XX/XX/XXXX XXXXFreeTime * XXXX XXXX WA This charge is tied to an application on an XXXX tablet. 
I do not own an XXXX tablet. 
There is no recurring charge attached to my XXXX account. 

The card provider has a secure messaging app for speaking to them in writing about issues. I wrote to them on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX. In the first message, I requested to start the process of disputing the charges, and I asked them to block the merchant / charges in the meantime. Without access to the offending XXXX account, I have no way to stop those charges. 

The amount is relatively small, somewhere around {$9.00} per month. I expected this to be a fairly simple process. 

They are refusing to engage with me in writing, directing me to call them. I've expressed that I am unable to call them, and repeated the issue, and offered the option to instead simply cancel my card. 

They responded with the same copy/paste message. In the meantime, they allowed the charge to occur AGAIN on XX/XX/XXXX, demonstrating that they aren't taking any action. 

I've attempted to go through the automated dispute process, and I am directed to report my card lost/stolen. When I then try to go through that process, I receive a website error. 

Screenshots in attached PDF.
